goal: Land the CodeRankEmbed (137M, Int8-quantised, ~137MB ONNX) default model on top of the foundational `OnnxSession` (P2-W8-F01 / WO-0058 — `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/onnx_inference.rs`). Master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787 ("CodeRankEmbed (137M, CPU) as default") + master-plan line 303 ("CPU-friendly, 50-150 embeddings/sec, ~137MB with Int8 quantization") + master-plan line 1332 (`embedding: vector[768]`) + master-plan line 2028-2029 (`embedding_model = "coderankembed"` config + `embedding_dimensions = 768`). Create NEW module `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs` declaring `pub struct CodeRankEmbed` whose `CodeRankEmbed::load(model_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, CodeRankEmbedError>` opens the on-disk Int8 ONNX model + the HuggingFace `tokenizers`-compatible `tokenizer.json` from `model_dir`, and `CodeRankEmbed::embed(&mut self, code: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, CodeRankEmbedError>` tokenizes the input code snippet and returns a 768-dim `Vec<f32>` via the underlying `OnnxSession::infer`. The frozen acceptance test `models::test_coderankembed_inference` lands at MODULE ROOT of `models.rs` per DEC-0007 (matching `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F02.acceptance_tests[0].selector = '-p ucil-embeddings models::test_coderankembed_inference'`), exercising real model load + real tokenization + real inference round-trip → asserts `result.len() == 768`. The model + tokenizer files (~137MB) are NOT committed; a NEW devtool installer `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` downloads them to `ml/models/coderankembed/{model.onnx,tokenizer.json}` from a pinned upstream mirror (HuggingFace `nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed` ONNX export — executor confirms exact filename + sha256 at research time). The verify script `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` runs the devtool installer first (idempotent — skips on existing sha256-matching files) then runs the cargo test. This unlocks P2-W8-F04 (LanceDB chunk indexing), P2-W8-F06 (throughput benchmark), P2-W8-F07 (vector query latency), and P2-W8-F08 (find_similar MCP tool).
plan_summary: Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs` (~200-280 LOC including module-level `//!` doc + `CodeRankEmbedError` + `CodeRankEmbed` impl + 1 frozen test). Add `tokenizers = { version = "0.20", default-features = false, features = ["onig", "esaxx_fast"] }` to workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` (alphabetical insertion); the `onig` + `esaxx_fast` features are `tokenizers`'s default Rust-only stack with no Python interop. Add `tokenizers.workspace = true` to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`. The `CodeRankEmbed` struct holds an `OnnxSession` (the F01 abstraction — `&mut self` constraint per WO-0058 lessons line 561) plus a `tokenizers::Tokenizer` plus the model directory path for tracing. `CodeRankEmbed::load(model_dir)` does (1) `let model_path = model_dir.join("model.onnx");` (2) `let session = OnnxSession::from_path(&model_path)?;` — the existing F01 entry point, (3) `let tokenizer_path = model_dir.join("tokenizer.json");` (4) `let tokenizer = tokenizers::Tokenizer::from_file(&tokenizer_path).map_err(|e| CodeRankEmbedError::Tokenizer { source: format!("{e}") })?;` (5) `Ok(Self { session, tokenizer, model_dir: model_dir.to_owned() })`. `CodeRankEmbed::embed(&mut self, code)` does (1) `let encoding = self.tokenizer.encode(code, true).map_err(...)?;` (2) `let token_ids: Vec<i64> = encoding.get_ids().iter().map(|id| i64::from(*id)).collect();` (3) `let raw = self.session.infer(&token_ids)?;` (4) IF the model emits per-token embeddings (3-D `[1, seq, 768]`), apply mean-pooling: `let dim = EMBEDDING_DIM; assert raw.len() % dim == 0; let n_tokens = raw.len() / dim; let mut pooled = vec![0f32; dim]; for tok in 0..n_tokens { for d in 0..dim { pooled[d] += raw[tok * dim + d]; } } for d in 0..dim { pooled[d] /= n_tokens as f32; } pooled` — OR if the model already pools (sentence-level 1-D `[768]`), pass-through. The executor inspects the actual model's output shape at first load and picks the right path (document inline in the impl). (5) Optionally L2-normalise: `let norm = (pooled.iter().map(|x| x*x).sum::<f32>()).sqrt(); pooled.iter().map(|x| x / norm.max(f32::EPSILON)).collect()`. (6) Return `Ok(normalised)`. The function takes `&mut self` because `OnnxSession::infer` takes `&mut self` per WO-0058 line 225. Add `pub const EMBEDDING_DIM: usize = 768;` per master-plan line 1332 + line 2029. `CodeRankEmbedError` variants: `Onnx { #[from] source: OnnxSessionError }`, `Tokenizer { source: String }` (boxed Tokenizer error opaque-ised to a String to avoid leaking the `tokenizers` crate's error type), `Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }`, `MissingModelFile { path: PathBuf }`, `DimensionMismatch { expected: usize, got: usize }`. The test `test_coderankembed_inference` is `#[test]` (sync — `OnnxSession::infer` is sync) at module root of `models.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Test exercises 5 sub-assertions: (SA1) `CodeRankEmbed::load` succeeds against `ml/models/coderankembed/`; (SA2) tokenizer encodes a non-empty code snippet to ≥1 token IDs; (SA3) `embed("fn hello() { println!(\"hi\"); }")` returns `Ok`; (SA4) the returned `Vec<f32>` has `.len() == 768` (the master-plan-pinned dimension); (SA5) the embedding contains finite floats (`.iter().all(|x| x.is_finite())`). Add `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` — idempotent installer that (a) detects if `ml/models/coderankembed/{model.onnx,tokenizer.json}` already exist with matching sha256 and exits 0, (b) otherwise downloads from a pinned upstream URL (executor picks `https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed/resolve/main/onnx/model_quantized.onnx` and `https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed/resolve/main/tokenizer.json` — confirm exact filenames at research time via `curl -I`; if upstream layout differs, document the actual URL in the script header AND in the ready-for-review note), (c) verifies sha256 against committed expected values (in the script header), (d) writes to `ml/models/coderankembed/`. Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` that runs the devtool installer first, then runs the cargo test. Pre-bake 3 mutation entries (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons): (M1) `load` body neutered to early `Err(CodeRankEmbedError::MissingModelFile { path: PathBuf::new() })` → SA1 panics; (M2) `embed` body neutered to early `Ok(Vec::new())` → SA4 panics on len mismatch; (M3) `EMBEDDING_DIM` literal value swap `768 → 100` → SA4 panics on len mismatch.
scope_in: Add to `Cargo.toml` workspace `[workspace.dependencies]` section, alphabetical position: `tokenizers = { version = "0.20", default-features = false, features = ["onig", "esaxx_fast"] }`. The `default-features = false` + named features avoids transitive HuggingFace HTTP / Python interop bloat — `onig` is the regex engine for BPE-style tokenization (CodeRankEmbed uses BPE; canonical for code embedders), `esaxx_fast` is the unigram-tokenizer suffix-array build. If the executor finds upstream `tokenizers 0.20` has churned a feature name, pick the closest semver (e.g. `0.21.x`) and document inline. The pin to `tokenizers 0.20` is a stable line as of 2026-Q1; confirm at research time via `cargo search tokenizers --limit 5`. Add to `crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml [dependencies]`: `tokenizers.workspace = true`. Existing entries (`ndarray.workspace = true`, `ort.workspace = true`, `thiserror.workspace = true`, `tokio.workspace = true`, `tracing.workspace = true`, `ucil-core = { path = "../ucil-core" }`) MUST keep their alphabetical order; insert `tokenizers.workspace = true` immediately before `thiserror.workspace = true` (alphabetical — t < t but `tokenizers` < `thiserror`? actually `thiserror` < `tokenizers` lexicographically since `i` < `o` at index 2 — verify the insertion point with `LC_ALL=C sort` semantics). AC02 verifies via `grep -E '^tokenizers\.workspace = true' crates/ucil-embeddings/Cargo.toml` and the `Cargo.toml` workspace-deps line.

Create NEW file `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs` (~200-280 LOC including doc + impl + 1 test). Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble cites: master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787 verbatim ("CodeRankEmbed (137M, CPU) as default, Qwen3-Embedding (8B, GPU optional) as upgrade"); master-plan line 303 ("CodeRankEmbed (137M params, MIT license, 8K context) ... CPU-friendly, 50-150 embeddings/sec, ~137MB with Int8 quantization"); master-plan line 1332 (`embedding: vector[768]`); master-plan line 2028-2029 (`embedding_model = "coderankembed"` + `embedding_dimensions = 768`); WO-0058 (the foundational `OnnxSession` this module composes over); the `&mut self` constraint inherited from `OnnxSession::infer` per WO-0058 lessons line 561 (consumers needing shared inference must wrap in `Arc<Mutex<_>>` or serialise via `tokio::sync::mpsc`); the deferral of P2-W8-F03 (Qwen3 GPU upgrade — separate `models::Qwen3Embedding` to land in a future WO), P2-W8-F04 (LanceDB indexing), P2-W8-F05 (chunker — produces the `&str` snippets `embed` consumes), P2-W8-F06 (throughput bench), P2-W8-F07 (vector latency bench), P2-W8-F08 (`find_similar` MCP tool). Include `# Examples` blocks per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. **Module-name discipline**: file name MUST be `models.rs` (NOT `coderankembed.rs` or `model.rs`) to match the frozen `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F02.acceptance_tests[0].selector = '-p ucil-embeddings models::test_coderankembed_inference'`.

Declare `pub const EMBEDDING_DIM: usize = 768;` at the module root of `models.rs` with rustdoc citing master-plan line 1332 + line 2029 explicitly. The constant is the master-plan-frozen dimension; mutation #3 (`768 → 100`) catches accidental dimension drift in future P2-W8-F04 / F05 / F08 consumers.

Declare `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)] #[non_exhaustive] pub enum CodeRankEmbedError` with variants: (a) `#[error("onnx session error: {source}")] Onnx { #[from] source: OnnxSessionError }` (auto-conversion from F01 errors via `?`); (b) `#[error("tokenizer error: {source}")] Tokenizer { source: String }` — the `tokenizers` crate's error type is opaque (`Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>`) and is unstable across minor versions; storing as `String` avoids version-coupling; (c) `#[error("io error: {source}")] Io { #[from] source: std::io::Error }`; (d) `#[error("required model file missing at {path:?}")] MissingModelFile { path: PathBuf }`; (e) `#[error("unexpected embedding dimension: expected {expected}, got {got}")] DimensionMismatch { expected: usize, got: usize }`. The `#[non_exhaustive]` attribute follows `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` libraries-use-thiserror invariant. Note: `Tokenizer` is NOT `#[from]` because the boxed-error → String conversion needs a `.map_err(|e| ...)` shim; document this rationale in the variant rustdoc.

Declare `pub struct CodeRankEmbed { session: OnnxSession, tokenizer: tokenizers::Tokenizer, model_dir: PathBuf }` with `#[derive(Debug)]` only (NOT `Clone` — `OnnxSession` is not `Clone` per WO-0058 line 108). The struct is `Send` (both `OnnxSession` and `tokenizers::Tokenizer` are `Send`). Document that consumers needing shared inference must wrap in `Arc<Mutex<CodeRankEmbed>>` per the `&mut self` plumbing — same pattern as the F01 `OnnxSession` rustdoc.

Implement `pub fn load(model_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, CodeRankEmbedError>` with this exact contract: (1) `let model_path = model_dir.join("model.onnx");` (2) `if !model_path.exists() { return Err(CodeRankEmbedError::MissingModelFile { path: model_path }); }` — the early existence check produces an operator-friendly error message instead of `OnnxSession::from_path`'s `ort::Error` wrapping; (3) `let session = OnnxSession::from_path(&model_path)?;` (auto-converts via `#[from]`); (4) `let tokenizer_path = model_dir.join("tokenizer.json");` (5) `if !tokenizer_path.exists() { return Err(CodeRankEmbedError::MissingModelFile { path: tokenizer_path }); }` (6) `let tokenizer = tokenizers::Tokenizer::from_file(&tokenizer_path).map_err(|e| CodeRankEmbedError::Tokenizer { source: format!("{e}") })?;` (7) `Ok(Self { session, tokenizer, model_dir: model_dir.to_owned() })`. Tracing: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.coderankembed.load", level = "debug", skip(model_dir), fields(model_dir = ?model_dir))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>`. The function is sync (matching `OnnxSession::from_path`).

Implement `pub fn embed(&mut self, code: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, CodeRankEmbedError>` with this contract: (1) `let encoding = self.tokenizer.encode(code, true).map_err(|e| CodeRankEmbedError::Tokenizer { source: format!("{e}") })?;` — the `true` adds special tokens (CLS/SEP) per CodeRankEmbed's training contract. (2) `let token_ids: Vec<i64> = encoding.get_ids().iter().map(|id| i64::from(*id)).collect();` — `tokenizers` returns `&[u32]`; convert to `i64` for the ONNX session input contract. (3) `let raw = self.session.infer(&token_ids)?;` — the F01 entry point; auto-converts `OnnxSessionError → Onnx` via `#[from]`. (4) Pool: detect output shape and apply mean-pooling if 3-D, pass-through if 1-D. The CodeRankEmbed ONNX export typically emits `[batch=1, seq=N, hidden=768]` flattened to `Vec<f32>` of length `1 * N * 768 = N * 768`; if `raw.len() == EMBEDDING_DIM` the model already pools (return as-is); otherwise mean-pool via `let n_tokens = raw.len() / EMBEDDING_DIM; if n_tokens == 0 || raw.len() % EMBEDDING_DIM != 0 { return Err(CodeRankEmbedError::DimensionMismatch { expected: EMBEDDING_DIM, got: raw.len() }); } let mut pooled = vec![0f32; EMBEDDING_DIM]; for tok in 0..n_tokens { for d in 0..EMBEDDING_DIM { pooled[d] += raw[tok * EMBEDDING_DIM + d]; } } let n_tokens_f = n_tokens as f32; for d in 0..EMBEDDING_DIM { pooled[d] /= n_tokens_f; }`. (5) L2-normalise: `let norm_sq: f32 = pooled.iter().map(|x| x * x).sum(); let norm = norm_sq.sqrt().max(f32::EPSILON); for d in 0..EMBEDDING_DIM { pooled[d] /= norm; }`. (6) `if pooled.len() != EMBEDDING_DIM { return Err(CodeRankEmbedError::DimensionMismatch { expected: EMBEDDING_DIM, got: pooled.len() }); }` — final invariant check. (7) `Ok(pooled)`. Tracing: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.embeddings.coderankembed.embed", level = "debug", skip(self, code), fields(code_len = code.len()))]`. Note: the executor MAY discover at runtime that CodeRankEmbed's ONNX export already outputs a pre-pooled `[1, 768]` (sentence-level pooling baked into the graph); in that case the `n_tokens == 1` path of step 4 fires (raw.len() == EMBEDDING_DIM), step 4's pooling is a no-op, step 5 still normalises. Document the actual observed output shape in the impl rustdoc + ready-for-review note.

Add the frozen acceptance test `#[test] fn test_coderankembed_inference()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The test selector `models::test_coderankembed_inference` matches the frozen `-p ucil-embeddings models::test_coderankembed_inference` substring per `feature-list.json:P2-W8-F02.acceptance_tests[0].selector`. Test body (5 sub-assertions in order):  
  **Setup**: locate the model directory via `let manifest_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")); let repo_root = manifest_dir.parent().expect("crates/ parent").parent().expect("repo root"); let model_dir = repo_root.join("ml").join("models").join("coderankembed");`. The path resolution uses `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` (per WO-0058 lessons line 545) — `crates/ucil-embeddings/` → `..` → `crates/` → `..` → repo root → `ml/models/coderankembed/`.  
  **Pre-flight**: if `model_dir.join("model.onnx").exists()` is `false`, emit a clear panic `panic!("CodeRankEmbed model not present at {model_dir:?}; run scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh first")` — the verify script `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` MUST run the devtool installer before this test (per scope_in[10]) so the panic is operator-friendly when run outside the verify script. **NO `return` early-skip / `#[ignore]`** — both are forbidden by the anti-laziness contract; a hard panic with an actionable message is the correct shape for a missing real-binary precondition (per WO-0055 lessons line 458 — runtime-skip via early `return` is functionally `#[ignore]` and was flagged as the wrong shape).  
  **(SA1) Model loads**: `let mut model = CodeRankEmbed::load(&model_dir).expect("CodeRankEmbed::load on real model");` — any `Err` panics with the underlying `CodeRankEmbedError` Debug-print so the verifier sees the actual failure mode (missing tokenizer.json, malformed ONNX, dim mismatch).  
  **(SA2) Tokenizer round-trip works**: `let probe = "fn main() { println!(\"hi\"); }"; let probe_encoding = model.tokenizer.encode(probe, true).expect("tokenizer encode"); assert!(!probe_encoding.get_ids().is_empty(), "tokenizer must produce ≥1 token IDs for non-empty code; got {:?}", probe_encoding.get_ids());` — proves the tokenizer.json loaded matches the model's expected vocab. (`pub` access to the `tokenizer` field is NOT required — gate via a `#[cfg(test)] pub(crate) fn tokenizer(&self) -> &tokenizers::Tokenizer` accessor on `CodeRankEmbed` for SA2 visibility, OR call `embed` and infer the assertion through SA3-SA5 — the executor picks the cleaner shape, document the choice.)  
  **(SA3) Embed succeeds**: `let embedding = model.embed("fn hello() { println!(\"hi\"); }").expect("embed succeeds on real Rust snippet");` — any `Err` panics with the full error chain.  
  **(SA4) Embedding dimension matches master-plan contract**: `assert_eq!(embedding.len(), 768, "CodeRankEmbed must emit 768-dim embeddings per master-plan §3.2 / §13 / §17.6 line 1332 + line 2029; got {} floats", embedding.len());` — this is the load-bearing assertion that mutation #3 (`EMBEDDING_DIM = 100`) catches.  
  **(SA5) Embedding values are finite**: `assert!(embedding.iter().all(|x| x.is_finite()), "all 768 floats must be finite (no NaN / ±Inf); got {} finite of {}", embedding.iter().filter(|x| x.is_finite()).count(), embedding.len());` — proves the model didn't emit garbage / overflow.  
  All assertions follow the `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", actual)` shape per WO-0042 lessons line 92 + WO-0051 lessons line 405 + WO-0055 lessons line 440 + WO-0058 line 547.

Update `crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs`: (a) add `pub mod models;` immediately after the existing `pub mod onnx_inference;` line (alphabetical: `models` < `onnx_inference`, so MOVE the `pub use` lines to after both `pub mod` declarations); the resulting block is `pub mod models;` then `pub mod onnx_inference;` then `pub use models::{CodeRankEmbed, CodeRankEmbedError, EMBEDDING_DIM};` then `pub use onnx_inference::{OnnxSession, OnnxSessionError};`. Verify post-edit via `grep -nE 'pub mod (models|onnx_inference);|pub use (models|onnx_inference)::' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/lib.rs | wc -l` returning `4`. (b) extend the existing module-level `//!` preamble (lines 1-10) with one trailing paragraph describing the `CodeRankEmbed` surface + the F03 (Qwen3) / F04 (LanceDB) / F05 (chunker) deferral pattern + master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787 citation. Cumulative re-export-discipline streak: 13 consecutive WOs cleared after WO-0058 (WO-0042 → WO-0058, excluding abandoned WO-0049 / WO-0054). This WO maintains the streak.

Add NEW file `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` per the standing devtool convention (model on `scripts/devtools/install-probe.sh` / `install-scip-rust.sh`). Script must: (a) `set -euo pipefail; IFS=$'\n\t'`; (b) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`; (c) target dir `ml/models/coderankembed/` (`mkdir -p`); (d) define `EXPECTED_MODEL_SHA256="<TBD-by-executor>"` and `EXPECTED_TOKENIZER_SHA256="<TBD-by-executor>"` — the executor sha256-sums the actual upstream files at first download and pins the hashes back into the script in a follow-up commit (per `Cargo.lock`-style pin-after-download discipline); (e) idempotency: if both files exist AND `sha256sum` matches expected, print `[OK] CodeRankEmbed already installed` and exit 0; (f) download via `curl -fL --retry 3 --retry-delay 2` from a pinned URL (executor confirms exact URL at research time — the leading candidate is `https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed/resolve/main/onnx/model_quantized.onnx` for the model and `https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed/resolve/main/tokenizer.json` for the tokenizer; if upstream layout differs, document the actual URL in the script header comment block AND in the ready-for-review note); (g) verify sha256 post-download; on mismatch, `rm` the file and `exit 1` with `[FAIL] sha256 mismatch on <file>: expected $EXPECTED, got $ACTUAL`; (h) `chmod +x scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh`. Header comment block cites WO-0059 + P2-W8-F02 + master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787 + the upstream URL + the canonical file layout. Include the `shellcheck` PATH-fallback pattern from WO-0044 onwards (`if command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1; then shellcheck "$0"; else echo "[INFO] shellcheck not on PATH; skipping shellcheck step."; fi`).

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` per the standing verify convention (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F01.sh` post-WO-0058). Script must: (a) `set -euo pipefail; IFS=$'\n\t'`; (b) `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"`; (c) **fail-fast on missing real binary** per WO-0055 lessons line 458: if `ml/models/coderankembed/model.onnx` does not exist, run `bash scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` (the installer is idempotent); if the installer exits non-zero, emit `[FAIL] P2-W8-F02: install-coderankembed.sh exited <code>` and `exit 1` BEFORE any cargo invocation — this is the operator-friendly shape that the verifier reads when network / upstream is unavailable; (d) verify the frozen selector lives at module root via `grep -nE '^fn test_coderankembed_inference|^pub fn test_coderankembed_inference' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs`; (e) run `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings models::test_coderankembed_inference -- --nocapture` and tee stdout to `/tmp/wo-0059-test.log`; (f) match either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests passed` shape via `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'` (per WO-0042 / WO-0048 / WO-0051 / WO-0055 / WO-0056 / WO-0058 lessons — same reusable shape); (g) on non-zero exit, emit `[FAIL] P2-W8-F02: <reason>` with a hint pointing at `ml/models/coderankembed/` + WO-0059 + master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 8 line 1787; (h) `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh`. Header comment block cites WO-0059 + P2-W8-F02 + the test selector verbatim. Include the `shellcheck` PATH-fallback pattern.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 + WO-0056 AC17 + WO-0058 AC15/16 — for `load` mutation (M1: early-return Err), the body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables)] { let _ = model_dir; return Err(CodeRankEmbedError::MissingModelFile { path: PathBuf::new() }); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder; for `embed` mutation (M2: empty Vec return), body becomes `#[allow(unused_variables)] { let _ = self; let _ = code; return Ok(Vec::new()); }` and `#[allow(unreachable_code)]` on the remainder. M3 (constant-mutation `EMBEDDING_DIM = 768 → 100`) is a literal-sed only — constants have no body to mutate; the existing match-arm-mutation precedent (WO-0048 / WO-0056 AC18) covers the shape. Document BOTH literal sed AND runtime-only variants in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC18 / AC19 / AC20).

**Pre-flight rustdoc grep before pushing** (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown` + WO-0058 scope_in[12]): `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-embeddings/src/models.rs` — every uppercase identifier in rustdoc paragraphs MUST be wrapped in backticks (`` `CodeRankEmbed` ``, `` `ONNX` ``, `` `LanceDB` ``, `` `BPE` ``, `` `CLS/SEP` ``, etc.). The crate sets `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]`; use plain backticks NOT shorthand `[Foo]` intra-doc links unless fully-qualified.

**Operator-readable assertion messages** (per WO-0051 lessons line 405 + WO-0055 line 440 + WO-0058 line 547): every `assert_eq!` / `assert!` in `test_coderankembed_inference` follows the shape `assert!(<predicate>, "<plain-English description>; got {:?}", <actual_value>)` so a verifier failure prints the actual observed value, not just the line number.

**Workspace-build precondition** (per WO-0055 line 456 + WO-0056 line 513 + WO-0058 line 514 standing rule): the executor MUST run `cargo build --workspace --tests` to exit 0 BEFORE writing the ready-for-review marker. Per-crate `cargo check -p ucil-embeddings` is NOT sufficient when adding a workspace dep because lib.rs re-export changes (and Cargo.lock churn) are visible to every downstream crate.

**Pre-test devtool install** is part of the executor's verification flow: BEFORE running `cargo test -p ucil-embeddings models::test_coderankembed_inference` locally, run `bash scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` to populate `ml/models/coderankembed/`. The 137MB download is one-time per machine; subsequent runs are instant. The verify script repeats this idempotently. Document the actual download time + sha256 fingerprints in the ready-for-review note (operator situational awareness for the verifier's first-run cargo-test wall-time inflation per WO-0058 line 565).

**Commit ladder — 6 commits** per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` and DEC-0005 module-coherence carve-out:  
  (1) `build(embeddings): add tokenizers workspace dep for HuggingFace tokenizer support` (~10 LOC: 1 workspace dep + 1 `[dependencies]` line),  
  (2) `feat(embeddings): add CodeRankEmbed skeleton with error type and constants` (~50 LOC: module preamble, `CodeRankEmbedError`, `CodeRankEmbed` struct, `EMBEDDING_DIM` constant),  
  (3) `feat(embeddings): implement CodeRankEmbed::load via OnnxSession + tokenizers::Tokenizer` (~50 LOC: `load` + tracing instrument + missing-file check),  
  (4) `feat(embeddings): implement CodeRankEmbed::embed with mean-pool + L2-norm` (~80 LOC: `embed` + tracing instrument + dimension invariants — DEC-0005 carve-out: pooling + normalisation form one coherent embedding-production unit),  
  (5) `test(embeddings): add models::test_coderankembed_inference frozen acceptance test` (~80 LOC: 5 sub-assertions + setup + pre-flight panic message — DEC-0005 carve-out: 5 sub-assertions form one regression-guard for the feature contract),  
  (6) `feat(embeddings): wire models into lib.rs preamble + add devtool installer + verify script` (~60 LOC: lib.rs re-exports + `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` + `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh`).  
  Each commit signed via the conventional `Phase: 2 / Feature: P2-W8-F02 / Work-order: WO-0059` trailer per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`. If commit-#5 stretches to ~150 LOC due to comprehensive sub-assertion shape, cite DEC-0005 + WO-0048's 297-LOC framing precedent + WO-0056's 542-LOC NEW-module precedent + WO-0058's 305-LOC commit precedent inline in the commit body per WO-0056 lessons line 502.

**Five upstream-API-shape adaptations precedent** (per WO-0058 lessons line 543): if `tokenizers 0.20` has shifted API surface since the WO was authored (e.g. `Tokenizer::from_file` returns a different error type, or `encoding.get_ids()` is renamed to `.ids()`), the executor adapts the call shape to fit upstream AND documents the divergence in the ready-for-review note + inline in the impl rustdoc — NO ADR required for purely-fitting upstream changes (per WO-0058 scope_out[6] precedent). If a NOVEL design choice surfaces (e.g. `tokenizers` 0.20 mandates async tokenization via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`), STOP and write an ADR before proceeding.
scope_out: **No Qwen3-Embedding GPU upgrade path.** P2-W8-F03 is the Qwen3 + GPU + Matryoshka-dimension feature (separate WO; the CodeRankEmbed pattern serves as the template). F02 stays CPU-only. The `ort` `cuda` / `tensorrt` / `directml` features are explicitly NOT enabled in the workspace dep — same as F01. F03's WO will gate them.

**No LanceDB integration.** P2-W8-F04 is the LanceDB chunk indexer that consumes `CodeRankEmbed::embed` outputs; F02 does NOT wire any database path. `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs` (WO-0053) is the LanceDB owner — F02 does NOT touch it.

**No chunker integration.** P2-W8-F05 is the embedding chunker (splits source files at tree-sitter function/class boundaries into ≤512-token chunks); F02 trusts callers to provide pre-chunked `&str` snippets. The `embed(&mut self, code: &str)` signature deliberately accepts a single snippet — chunking is the F05 layer.

**No `find_similar` MCP tool.** P2-W8-F08 is the MCP-exposed `find_similar` tool; F02 ships only the embedding production primitive. F08 will compose `CodeRankEmbed::embed` over LanceDB vector search at the daemon's MCP handler.

**No throughput / latency benchmarks.** P2-W8-F06 (throughput ≥50 emb/s on CPU) and P2-W8-F07 (vector query p95 <100ms warm) are benchmark WOs; F02 ships only the correctness-asserting frozen test (1 sample input → 768 floats out, finite). F06's criterion bench harness lands separately.

**No async API surface.** `OnnxSession::infer` is sync (`&mut self` per WO-0058 line 225); `CodeRankEmbed::embed(&mut self, code)` mirrors that contract. The natural async wrap is `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || model.embed(code))` at the consumer site — that pattern lands in F04 / F08 where the tokio runtime is in play. F02's `CodeRankEmbed` MUST stay sync so the test runs in `#[test]` (NOT `#[tokio::test]`); same shape as F01.

**No `ndarray 0.16 → 0.17` workspace bump.** WO-0058 lessons line 555 named F02 / F05 as candidate sites for aligning `ndarray` to `0.17` to drop the duplicate (workspace `0.16` vs `ort 2.0.0-rc.12`'s internal `0.17`); F02 explicitly defers this — the duplicate is non-blocking per WO-0058 verifier accept (line 570), and bumping `ndarray` could ripple through `crates/ucil-treesitter/`'s consumers. Document the deferral as a follow-up trigger in the ready-for-review note.

**No interaction with WO-0057.** WO-0057 (in flight; P2-W7-F06 `search_code` G2 fused) modifies `crates/ucil-daemon/{server.rs, plugin_manager.rs, lib.rs, g2_search.rs (NEW)}` per its allow-list. WO-0059's allow-list is disjoint (`crates/ucil-embeddings/**` + `Cargo.toml` + `Cargo.lock` + `ml/models/coderankembed/.gitignore` + `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` + `scripts/verify/P2-W8-F02.sh` only). The two WOs are safe to run in parallel; merge order does not matter.

**No new ADR.** DEC-0005 (module-coherence), DEC-0006 (download-binaries / pre-built test-time binary precedent — applied transitively to the upstream HuggingFace ONNX download via `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh`), DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement) all cover the WO. If a NOVEL pattern surfaces (e.g. `tokenizers 0.20` mandates async, or the model output shape requires a non-standard pooling strategy not anticipated in scope_in[7]), STOP and write an ADR before working around.
**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` (M1, M2, M3 per scope_in[12]) are the authoritative anti-laziness layer.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`, `tests/integration/**`, `tests/fixtures/**`** — F02 is single-crate (`ucil-embeddings`), single-source-file (NEW `models.rs`) plus `lib.rs` re-exports + the devtool installer + the verify script + the `ml/models/coderankembed/.gitignore` (declares the dir-tree but excludes the 137MB binary itself). The `ml/models/coderankembed/` directory's existence on disk is created by the installer; only `.gitignore` is committed.

**No commit of the 137MB model.onnx or tokenizer.json.** Both are downloaded by `scripts/devtools/install-coderankembed.sh` and gitignored. `git ls-files ml/models/coderankembed/` MUST return only `.gitignore` post-WO. The installer's sha256 pins are the integrity surface (per `Cargo.lock`-style discipline).

**No `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies, runtime test-skip via early `return`** — all forbidden by root CLAUDE.md + WO-0055 lessons line 458. Missing-binary precondition uses `panic!` with operator-friendly message + verify-script fail-fast PRE-cargo-test (per scope_in[10]).

**No mock of `ort::Session`, `ort::SessionBuilder`, `ort::Value`, `ndarray::Array`, `tokenizers::Tokenizer`, or `OnnxSession`.** ONNX Runtime + tokenizers are critical deps per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` (no mocks of Serena, LSP, SQLite, LanceDB, Docker, ONNX Runtime); the test uses a real CodeRankEmbed model + real tokenizer + real `OnnxSession::infer`. WO-0058 lessons line 542 confirms the pattern.
**No `git commit --no-verify`, `--amend` after push, or force-push** — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md.

**No commit-subject lines exceeding 70 chars** (per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md` AC20). The 6-commit ladder in scope_in[16] uses subject templates that all fit ≤70 chars; if a late-stage cleanup commit is needed and would exceed, document the constraint chain in the ready-for-review note per WO-0058 line 568 precedent.
